stage_summary: Stage 8 adds repository-wide readers only after stewardship exists. The current repo has no `status` command, no `doctor` command, and no visible Domum reader surfaces. The rebased implementation plan limits this stage to disk-truth readers: status, doctor, maturity classification, health warnings, continuity reporting, and provenance-aware repository interpretation grounded in visible files rather than orchestration theater. Because Stage 7 stewardship does not exist yet, every Stage 8 deliverable remains future work and depends on archive-aware reading surfaces first.
baseline_guardrails: Stage 1 Finis baseline is already implemented and remains the canonical goal layer.
Stage 2 Formam baseline is already implemented and remains the canonical deliverable and map layer.
Stage 3 Agenda baseline is already implemented and remains the canonical task, DAG, and task-log layer.
Post-baseline Stage 8 deliverables must report on visible repository truth after stewardship exists; they must not recreate the baseline engines or invent orchestration that bypasses on-disk state.

Stage 8 must preserve reader boundaries: status, doctor, maturity, continuity, and health reporting belong here, while goal shaping, artifact shaping, task shaping, and stewardship mutation remain owned by Finis, Formam, Agenda, and Domum.

deliverables.title: Repository status command
deliverables.description: Add a repo-wide `status` reader that summarizes the current visible AZQ state from on-disk artifacts across the live engines and stewardship surfaces. Status must report what is visibly true now, not what the architecture hopes is true in spirit.
deliverables.why_it_exists: The implementation plan explicitly names `status` as a Stage 8 reader, and the current CLI surface stops at `agenda` with no repository-wide reporting command. AZQ needs a readable answer to the question: what state am I actually in right now?
acceptance_criteria: The CLI exposes a status reader that reports across Scintilla, Finis, Formam, Agenda, and any live stewardship artifacts.
The status output is derived from visible files and identifiers on disk rather than hidden process memory.
The status reader distinguishes implemented code capability from the narrower checked-in artifact snapshot when relevant.
The status reader distinguishes active canonical artifacts from archived artifacts where stewardship data exists.
Tests verify at least one end-to-end repository status report against deterministic fixture state.

deliverables.title: Repository doctor command
deliverables.description: Add a repo-wide `doctor` reader that flags visible contradictions, missing links, stale archive evidence, and broken continuity across canonical AZQ artifacts. Doctor must remain read-only and diagnostic, not a stealth repair script in a lab coat.
deliverables.why_it_exists: The implementation plan names `doctor` as a Stage 8 reader, and there is currently no command that validates cross-engine storage truth in the live repo. AZQ needs a way to say not just what exists, but what is sick, stale, or broken.
acceptance_criteria: The CLI exposes a doctor reader that can detect at least missing parent references, stale archive markers, contradictory status surfaces, or malformed canonical identifiers from visible files.
Doctor findings cite exact artifact paths or identifiers so the operator can inspect the problem on disk.
Doctor reports remain read-only and do not silently repair repository state.
Doctor output distinguishes hard inconsistencies from softer warnings when evidence is incomplete or mixed.
Tests cover at least one broken fixture state and one healthy fixture state.

deliverables.title: Repository maturity and continuity report
deliverables.description: Add a reader surface that classifies repository maturity and continuity from visible artifacts, including where code capability exceeds the checked-in data snapshot and where continuity is preserved only partially across active and archived state.
deliverables.why_it_exists: The rebased implementation plan stresses the distinction between code capability and checked-in artifact truth, and Stage 8 is where that should become a visible reader rather than hidden interpretation. AZQ should be able to explain not just what exists, but how coherent the chain of continuity really is.
acceptance_criteria: A repo-wide reader can state which stages are represented by code capability and which are represented by visible artifacts.
The report cites concrete evidence such as artifact directories, canonical files, archive manifests, or missing artifact families.
Continuity reporting remains conservative when evidence is mixed or partial rather than overstating completion.
The report distinguishes active continuity from archive-preserved continuity when both are present.
Tests or fixtures demonstrate output for a repo state where code capability and checked-in data differ.

deliverables.title: Visible repository health warnings
deliverables.description: Add a reader surface for warning about visible repo drift such as stale docs versus live code, missing canonical directories, broken archive contracts, or continuity gaps that do not yet rise to full doctor-level inconsistency.
deliverables.why_it_exists: Stage 8 is defined as a disk-truth reporting stage, and the current repo already demonstrates real doc-versus-code tension that later readers should surface plainly. AZQ needs warnings, not just verdicts.
acceptance_criteria: Reader output can flag at least one class of doc-versus-repo contradiction or canonical storage inconsistency from visible files.
Warnings are reviewable and cite the specific file or path evidence that triggered them.
Warnings remain separate from remediation tasks and do not silently mutate repository state.
Warnings distinguish advisory drift from harder repository breakage when both may appear in the same repo.
Tests cover at least one warning-producing fixture and one clean fixture.

deliverables.title: Provenance-preserving repository reader evidence
deliverables.description: Add a repository-reader provenance contract that preserves the boundary between active canonical artifacts, archived artifacts, documentation assertions, code-capability interpretation, and reader-generated report text. This contract should make it possible to understand how a reader reached its conclusion without letting summary output masquerade as canonical repository truth.
deliverables.why_it_exists: AZQ is meant to protect human orientation while working with LLMs and complex repo state. Stages 4 through 7 established provenance boundaries for intent, artifact shaping, task shaping, and stewardship; Stage 8 must preserve that discipline at the reader layer so status and doctor output remain evidence-backed interpretation instead of accidental lore.
acceptance_criteria: Stage 8 reader artifacts or outputs expose enough structure to distinguish active canonical artifact evidence, archived artifact evidence, documentation evidence, and reader-generated interpretation.
The provenance contract preserves exact linkage to visible paths, canonical identifiers, and archive-aware evidence where applicable.
No Stage 8 reader output presents documentation claims, code capability assumptions, or prior report summaries as canonical repository truth without citing the underlying visible evidence.
Reader-generated summaries remain clearly identified as report text rather than canonical artifact state.
Tests or review fixtures demonstrate one end-to-end example where reader conclusions remain visibly tied to underlying repo evidence across active, archived, and mixed states.
